criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented async def create_pr(worktree, title, body, base='main', draft=False) -> PRResult in src/forge/adapters/gh/operations.py (turn 1, kept as-is). PRResult is imported from forge.adapters.git.models per the shared adapter return contract (API-subprocess.md §4). The unit-test class TestCreatePrReturnContract has four asserts confirming PRResult is returned on missing-credentials, success, non-zero-exit, and exception paths; the BDD-key-example wiring also asserts isinstance(result, PRResult) on the happy path.
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented async def create_pr(worktree, title, body, base='main', draft=False) -> PRResult in src/forge/adapters/gh/operations.py (turn 1, kept as-is). PRResult is imported from forge.adapters.git.models per the shared adapter return contract (API-subprocess.md §4). The unit-test class TestCreatePrReturnContract has four asserts confirming PRResult is returned on missing-credentials, success, non-zero-exit, and exception paths; the BDD-key-example wiring also asserts isinstance(result, PRResult) on the happy path.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Pre-flight check: if `GH_TOKEN` is unset (or empty) in `os.environ`,
criteria_verification.evidence: create_pr reads os.environ.get('GH_TOKEN', '') on every call and returns the canonical missing-credentials PRResult before reaching _execute. Three unit tests (test_missing_gh_token_returns_missing_credentials_failure, test_empty_gh_token_is_treated_as_missing, test_gh_token_is_re_read_on_every_call) cover unset/empty/changed-between-calls. The BDD @negative scenario 'A pull-request creation without GitHub credentials returns a structured error' is now wired in tests/bdd/test_guardkit_command_invocation_engine.py and also asserts execute_mock.assert_not_called() / assert_not_awaited() to prove no PR is created.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: On success, parse the PR URL from gh's stdout and populate
criteria_verification.evidence: _build_command() assembles ['gh', 'pr', 'create', '--title', title, '--body', body, '--base', base] and appends '--draft' when draft=True. _execute is invoked with command=command, cwd=str(worktree). Unit tests test_default_command_shape_uses_separate_tokens and test_draft_flag_appends_dash_dash_draft assert the exact argv shape including --base override. The BDD @key-example scenario 'Forge opens a pull request for the build through the version-control adapter' now exercises the full command path and asserts command[0]=='gh', command[1:3]==['pr','create'], '--base' present and pointing at the configured branch, and cwd == str(worktree).

criteria_verification.evidence: _PR_URL_PATTERN = re.compile(r'https://github\.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/pull/(\d+)') extracts URL via group 0 and PR number via int(group(1)). Unit tests test_success_parses_pr_url_and_number_from_stdout and test_pr_number_parsed_from_trailing_slash_component verify owner/repo/pull/123 and pull/4567 with multiline stdout. The BDD @key-example scenario asserts result.pr_url == expected_url and result.pr_number == 77.

criteria_verification.evidence: _execute is wrapped in try/except Exception as exc returning PRResult(status='failed', stderr=f'{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}'). criteria_verification.criterion_text: Unit tests: missing GH_TOKEN, success path with parsed URL, non-zero
criteria_verification.evidence: _build_command places title and body as their own list elements; _execute uses asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(*command, ...) which spawns the binary directly without a shell. Unit tests test_backticks_in_body_pass_through_literal, test_dollar_signs_in_body_pass_through_literal, and test_metacharacters_in_title_pass_through_literal assert that 'Run `rm -rf /` to test', 'Set $HOME and $PATH explicitly', and 'Fix `bug` in $service && rm tmp' appear verbatim as a single list token preceded by --body or --title.
criteria_verification.notes: _build_command places title and body as their own list elements; _execute uses asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(*command, ...) which spawns the binary directly without a shell.
